title: Handle puppeteer-extra global plugin state — dedupe + document caveat
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:45 calls puppeteer.use(plugin) per module options. puppeteer-extra is a global singleton so plugins from different forRoot() calls bleed across browsers. Repeated module re-registration in tests stacks plugins. At minimum: dedupe (skip if already registered) and add a JSDoc warning on PuppeteerModuleOptions.plugins. Investigate per-browser scoping if puppeteer-extra exposes a non-singleton API.

title: Verify and fix multi-browser shutdown closes every Browser instance
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:103-114 onApplicationShutdown resolves 'getBrowserToken(this.options)' from its own moduleRef. With multiple PuppeteerCoreModule instances, each instance closes only its own browser. Add a multi-browser e2e spec asserting both browsers actually disconnect on app close. Fix any leak found.
notes: Investigation: added tests/e2e/puppeteer-multi-browser.spec.ts covering both forRoot and forRootAsync with two distinct browser instances. Verified via browser.process().pid that Puppeteer spawns separate Chromium processes per instance and that browser.connected flips to false on app.close() for both. No leak found: each PuppeteerCoreModule instance receives its own scoped PUPPETEER_MODULE_OPTIONS, so getBrowserToken(this.options) in onApplicationShutdown resolves the correct per-instance browser token. Closing as verified-by-test.
close_reason: Verified by new e2e spec; per-instance shutdown works correctly.

title: Open PR for puppeteer/v24 branch and verify semantic-release major bump
description:
After both feat commits land on the puppeteer/v24 branch, open the PR against master and validate the release pipeline:

- CI matrix (Node 20/22/24 under aa-exec --profile=chrome) must be green.
- Public API surface check: node -e "const m=require('./dist'); console.log(Object.keys(m).sort())" must list everything except PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS and PuppeteerModuleOptions.plugins.
- semantic-release dry-run output in CI logs should announce a MAJOR bump driven by the two BREAKING CHANGE footers (v3.0.0).
- PR description should call out the two breaks for downstream users: peer dep narrowed to ^22||^23||^24 (drop v21, add v24); puppeteer-extra plugin path removed (pin to 2.x branch if needed).

This is the consumer-facing deliverable; until the PR merges and master ships, the branch work is not done.

title: feat(deps)!: drop puppeteer-extra integration
description:
Remove the puppeteer-extra plugin code path entirely. puppeteer-extra (and puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth) have not published since 2023-03-01 (~3 years); Snyk's advisor flags the project as inactive. Stealth evasions target old-headless and have not been re-tuned for the new-headless default introduced in puppeteer v22. Community has moved to rebrowser-puppeteer for serious stealth.

This is a public-API break (drops PuppeteerModuleOptions.plugins, PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS constant, puppeteer-extra peer dep). Users who still need plugins are pointed at the 2.x branch in the README.

Files:
- package.json: remove peerDependencies.puppeteer-extra; remove devDependencies.puppeteer-extra and devDependencies.puppeteer-extra-plugin-stealth.
- lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts: import puppeteer from 'puppeteer' (not 'puppeteer-extra'); delete PuppeteerExtraPlugin import, pluginRegistrationLogger, registerPlugins helper, PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS import, and the pluginProvider blocks in forRoot/forRootAsync.
- lib/puppeteer.constants.ts: delete PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS export.
- lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts: delete PuppeteerExtraPlugin import and the plugins?: PuppeteerExtraPlugin[] field with its JSDoc.
- tests/e2e/puppeteer-with-plugin.spec.ts: delete file.
- tests/src/app-with-plugin.module.ts: delete file.
- tests/src/base/base.controller.ts: delete the @Get('stealth-check') handler (the only consumer of arh.antoinevastel.com bot test).
- tests/e2e/puppeteer.spec.ts: delete the PUPPETEER_DISABLE_HEADLESS_WARNING env-flag line (v21 deprecation warning, no longer emitted on v22+).
- README.md: drop puppeteer-extra from install command; remove plugins row from options table; add callout pointing legacy users at the 2.x branch and recommending rebrowser-puppeteer for new projects.

Verification:
- npm install && npm run build && npm run lint && npm run test:e2e (6 remaining specs).
- grep -r puppeteer-extra lib/ tests/ README.md → zero matches.
- grep -r PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS lib/ tests/ → zero matches.
- grep -r PuppeteerExtraPlugin lib/ tests/ → zero matches.
- Diff dist/index.d.ts exports vs baseline: only PUPPETEER_BROWSER_PLUGINS and PuppeteerModuleOptions.plugins should disappear.

Commit message: feat(deps)!: drop puppeteer-extra integration
With BREAKING CHANGE footer.

title: feat(deps): bump puppeteer peer range to ^22 || ^23 || ^24
description:
Drop puppeteer ^21 from the peer-dep range and add v24 (currently latest 24.43.0, GA 2025-01-10), so the supported range becomes ^22 || ^23 || ^24. Also bump the dev dep to puppeteer ^24 and refresh stale docs that still reference removed APIs.

The library is already structurally insulated (uses Parameters<PuppeteerNode['launch']>[0] and only Browser/Page instance APIs), so no logic changes are needed — but README and JSDoc still show headless: 'new' (gone since v22) and PuppeteerNodeLaunchOptions (renamed to LaunchOptions in v24). Those have to be corrected for type-safety on v24.

Files:
- package.json: peerDependencies.puppeteer ^21||^22||^23 → ^22||^23||^24; devDependencies.puppeteer ^23 → ^24; refresh package-lock.json.
- lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts: update the LaunchOptions JSDoc range; refresh the plugins-block headless comment (headless: 'new' no longer exists; for puppeteer-extra plugin compat use headless: 'shell').
- README.md: replace 'New Headless' callout with current-state note; replace every headless: 'new' with headless: true; replace every PuppeteerNodeLaunchOptions with LaunchOptions.

Verification:
- npm install && npm run build && npm run lint && npm run test:e2e (all 7 specs incl. stealth-check).
- npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json (sanity-check launch-options derivation on v24).

Commit message: feat(deps): bump puppeteer peer range to ^22 || ^23 || ^24
With BREAKING CHANGE footer noting v21 drop and headless: 'new' / PuppeteerNodeLaunchOptions removal.

title: Tune up CI workflows: release.yml + pull_request.yml
description:
Both GitHub Actions workflows have drifted from current repo layout and modern CI practice.

release.yml is broken at job-time:
- runs npm test (no such script in package.json)
- npm clean-install in test/ directory which does not exist
- extra_plugins: @semantic-release/github duplicates plugin already in .releaserc
- triggers on beta and *.x branches that .releaserc does not configure

pull_request.yml drifted:
- Node 21 in matrix (EOL)
- job named 'test' actually only does lint and audit; lint depends on test_matrix unnecessarily
- push: branches: [renovate/**] trigger duplicates the pull_request trigger Renovate already fires
- coverage re-runs full Chromium e2e on every PR just to upload Codecov

Decisions (confirmed with user):
- Trust merge queue; release runs build + publish only
- Trim release branches to master + next
- Node matrix: 20, 22, 24
- Coverage only on merge_group

Plan file: ~/.claude/plans/i-did-some-changes-mellow-hare.md

title: Audit major dependency bumps flagged by ncu
description:
Several deps have major-version bumps available. Each needs review of breaking changes and CI impact before applying:

- @commitlint/cli, @commitlint/config-angular: 18 → 21
- @types/jest: 29 → 30 (paired with jest 30)
- @types/node: ^20 → ^25
- @types/supertest: 6 → 7 (paired with supertest 7)
- @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin, @typescript-eslint/parser: 6 → 8
- eslint: 8 → 10 (flat config migration required)
- eslint-config-prettier: 9 → 10
- jest: 29 → 30
- lint-staged: ^15 → ^17
- supertest: 6 → 7
- typescript: ^5 → ^6

NOTE: puppeteer ^23 → ^24 is being handled separately and is NOT in scope for this issue.

Applied safe bumps in nestjs-puppeteer-qim.

title: Dependency sanity check and bumps
description: Audit dependencies, devDependencies, and peerDependencies in package.json against the current registry. Identify outdated packages (npm outdated). For each: classify as safe-bump (patch/minor) vs major (needs review). Apply safe bumps in one commit; flag majors with a short note for the user to decide. Re-run lint, build, and e2e after bumping. Do NOT bump peerDependency ranges without confirming the public-API impact.

title: Validate forRootAsync requires one of useFactory/useClass/useExisting
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:145-147 type-asserts (options.useClass || options.useExisting) as Type — if all three of useFactory/useClass/useExisting are missing, the inject array contains undefined and DI fails with a confusing error. Throw an explicit 'PuppeteerModule.forRootAsync requires one of useFactory, useClass, or useExisting' early.

title: Tighten public types — drop any from forRoot and async options
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:35 has 'forRoot(options: any = {})' — should be 'PuppeteerModuleOptions'. lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts:33-34 has 'useFactory?: (...args: any[])' and 'inject?: any[]' — should match @nestjs/typeorm convention using InjectionToken[] / OptionalFactoryDependency. Pure type-tightening, no runtime change.

title: Modernize puppeteer launch-options type for v23 compatibility
description: lib/interfaces/puppeteer-options.interface.ts uses Partial<PuppeteerNodeLaunchOptions>. Puppeteer v23 renamed this to LaunchOptions. Pick a type strategy that works across the peer-dep range '^21 || ^22 || ^23' without breaking consumers. Verify in a clean install against each major.

title: Detect duplicate browser name registration
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:54,84 registers a browser provider keyed by getBrowserToken(options). Two forRoot() calls with the same 'name' silently overwrite the DI token — no warning. Throw a clear error or log a warning when a duplicate name is detected (track registered names in a module-level Set).

title: Improve onApplicationShutdown error logging (preserve stack)
description: lib/puppeteer-core.module.ts:111-113 currently logs 'e?.message' only — drops stack and breaks for non-Error throws. Pass the full error to this.logger.error so the Nest logger can format it properly.
